content.full_text:
Placer County Counsel
175 FULWEILER AVENUE, ROOM 301 — COUNTY ADMINISTRATIVE CENTER
AUBURN, CALIFORNIA 95603-4581 • (916) 889-4044

April 18, 1990

Cathy Donovan
FPPC Bulletin
Legal Division
428 J Street, Suite 800
Sacramento, CA 95814

Dear Cathy:

     Placer County again requests an opinion of your staff on a
matter of potential conflict of interest.

FACTS:

     The Board of Supervisors of Placer County employs several
individuals as aides or assistants to the Board of Supervisors.
These individuals are employed on a contract basis and perform
services directly for individual members of the Board, and for
the Board as a body. A copy of one such contract is enclosed.
They are paid out of the budget of the Board of Supervisors.

     The spouse of one such individual is a member of the
1989-1990 Placer County Grand Jury. As such, she is in a
position to direct county involved in investigations of County
departments and officials, including the Board of Supervisors.
Of particular concern in this instance is the fact that the
contract employee is presently working exclusively for one
Supervisor, and fear has been expressed that the individual may
have the ability to influence Grand Jury recommendations and
criticisms through his spouse, aimed at other Supervisors and
elected officials. Thus it may be possible for the contract
employee to enhance his position by causing Grand Jury criticism
of his employer's political opponents and rivals on the Board of
Supervisors, and throughout County government.

QUESTION:

     Is it a conflict of interest for the spouse of an
individual who is a contract employee of the Board of
Supervisors, and who performs services exclusively for one
Supervisor, to serve on the Grand Jury and participate in
investigations of the Board of Supervisors?

REQUEST:

We request an advice letter on this subject, if possible,
as soon as you may reasonably provide one. We will be happy to
provide such information and documents as you might need to
resolve this question.

Sincerely,

[signature]
RICHARD K. DENHALTER,
County Counsel

DESCRIPTION: CONTRACT SERVICES                CONTRACT NO. 9184
OR FIELD DEPUTY FOR                           BEGINS:___11/20/89__
THE AUBURN AREA.                              ENDS:_____6/30/90___
                                              ADMINISTERING
                                              AGENCY:____BOS______

1
2
3
4         THIS AGREEMENT MADE AND ENTERED into this __7th__ day of
5   November, 1989, BY AND BETWEEN the County of Placer,
6   hereinafter referred to as "COUNTY", and GREG JOHNSON_________,
7   hereinafter referred to as "FIELD DEPUTY".

8                            W I T N E S S E T H:

9         1.  SCOPE OF WORK.  COUNTY hereby retains the above as an
10  FIELD DEPUTY to provide to the individual members of the Board
11  of Supervisors professional and technical advice and assistance
12  relating to the discharge of their supervisorial duties as it
13  relates primarily to District 3 as follows:

14      2.  TERM OF CONTRACT.  The term of this Contract shall be
15  for a period commencing on November 20, 1989, and ending June
16  30, 1990.

17      3.  WORK ASSIGNMENTS.  The COUNTY, through its Board and/or
18  District 3 Supervisor, shall furnish FIELD DEPUTY with work
19  assignments of such nature and frequency as is appropriate.

20      4.  COUNTY shall furnish such office space and equipment as
21  it deems appropriate.

22      5.  AMOUNT OF PAYMENT.  FIELD DEPUTY agrees to provide
23  services for a total payment not to exceed $6,200.00 in
24  salary.  The hourly rate shall be $10.10 per hour.  In
25  addition, the COUNTY agrees to reimburse for actual and
26  necessary expenses related to the performance of this
27  agreement.  Mileage shall be reimbursed at the rate of thirty
28  (30) cents per mile, not to exceed $150.00 per month.

29      6.  All work product of the FIELD DEPUTY shall be and
30  remain the property of the COUNTY OF PLACER.

31      7.  PROGRESS REPORTS.  FIELD DEPUTY shall maintain, at all
32  times, complete detailed records with regard to his work, and
33  such records shall be in a form approved by COUNTY.

34      8.  PAYMENT SCHEDULE.  Claims for compensation shall be
35  submitted once a month not later than the 5th day of each
36  month, for work done the preceding calendar month.

37      9.  Nothing herein shall, in any way, be deemed to create
38  the relationship of Employer-Employee, but rather such
39  relationship shall be that of Client-Independent Contractor.
40  Contractor FIELD DEPUTY shall be solely responsible for the

41                              -1-

salaries and other applicable benefits, including Worker's
Compensation, of all agents or employees employed to perform
Contractor's obligations under this Agreement.

10.  HOLD HARMLESS.
     a. FIELD DEPUTY agrees to save harmless and indemnify
COUNTY from every claim or demand of any kind or nature
whatsoever which may be made by any person resulting from the
action or inaction of FIELD DEPUTY or her officers, agents or
employees under the terms of this agreement, and further
agrees, at his own cost, expense and risk to defend any and all
actions, suits, or other legal proceedings which may be brought
against COUNTY as a result of such action or inaction, which
further agrees that he will pay or satisfy any judgment which
many be rendered therein.

     b. In such event that claim or demand of any kind or
nature whatsoever is made by any person against action or
inaction of the COUNTY, and in so doing that person includes
then COUNTY agrees to defend any and all actions, suits, or
other legal proceedings which may be brought against this FIELD
DEPUTY as a result of such action or inaction, and will pay or
satisfy any judgment which may be rendered therein.

11.  NON-ASSIGNABILITY.  This agreement, and the rights
and duties thereunder, shall not be assigned in whole or in
part without the express written consent of COUNTY.

12.  INSURANCE.  SECOND PARTY shall, contemporaneous with
the signing of this agreement, file with the COUNTY a
Certificate of Insurance showing automobile liability coverage
in the amount of not less than  $100/300,000   Bodily Injury;
and  $25,000      Property Damage.

13.  CANCELLATION.   This agreement may be cancelled by
either party upon the giving of thirty days advance written
notice.  Such notice shall be personally served or given by
United States Mail.  In the event of cancellation by COUNTY,
FIELD DEPUTY shall be paid for all work performed to the date
of cancellation.

     IN WITNESS WHEREOF, the parties hereto have executed this
agreement the day and year first above written.

                         COUNTY OF PLACER

     BY: _________________ _________________
         Chairman, Board of Supervisors

     FIELD DEPUTY

     BY: _________________ _________________
         Greg Johnson

     _________________ _________________
     Richard Denhalter, County Counsel
                                    "7/3/89

     ATTACHMENT:  CERTIFICATE OF INSURANCE

sections.question:
Is it a conflict of interest for the spouse of an individual who is a contract employee of the Board of Supervisors, and who performs services exclusively for one Supervisor, to serve on the Grand Jury and participate in investigations of the Board of Supervisors?

REQUEST:

We request an advice letter on this subject, if possible, as soon as you may reasonably provide one. We will be happy to provide such information and documents as you might need to resolve this question.
sections.facts:
The Board of Supervisors of Placer County employs several individuals as aides or assistants to the Board of Supervisors. These individuals are employed on a contract basis and perform services directly for individual members of the Board, and for the Board as a body. A copy of one such contract is enclosed. They are paid out of the budget of the Board of Supervisors.

The spouse of one such individual is a member of the 1989-1990 Placer County Grand Jury. As such, she is in a position to be directly involved in investigations of County departments and officials, including the Board of Supervisors. Of particular concern in this instance is the fact that the contract employee is presently working exclusively for one Supervisor, and fear has been expressed that the individual may have the ability to influence Grand Jury recommendations and criticisms through his spouse, aimed at other Supervisors and elected officials. Thus it may be possible for the contract employee to enhance his position by causing Grand Jury criticism of his employer's political opponents and rivals on the Board of Supervisors, and throughout County government.
